title: Exhaust Gas Recirculation System Structure - Overview

blocks.text: Exhaust Gas Recirculation System Structure - Overview

An exhaust gas recirculation system (EGR system) is used to lower the maximum combustion temperature, improve fuel economy, and reduce NOx levels. A portion of the exhaust gas is taken from directly after the direct catalytic converter and introduced directly into the intake manifold via the EGR valve. The EGR valve is electrically operated, and the PGM-FI ECU controls the lift according to the driving conditions to adjust the amount of exhaust gas reduction. The EGR valve is electrically operated. The lift sensor integrated with the EGR valve sends the lift of the EGR valve to the PGM-FI ECU for feedback control of the target lift.

Exhaust gas taken out from just after the direct catalytic converter is sent through the EGR pipe to the EGR valve, where it is adjusted to the optimum volume through the cylinder head passage and introduced into the intake manifold through the EGR joint mounted on the cylinder head.
